title: 10 best herbal tinctures for open pores

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with open pores because of its unique ability to gently balance and regulate the skin's natural moisture levels. The aloe vera extract in the tincture works to tighten and refine the pores, reducing their appearance and leaving the skin looking smoother and more refined. Additionally, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in the aloe help to soothe and calm the skin, further reducing inflammation and congestion that can lead to enlarged pores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling, allowing pores to breathe and open up, improving skin clarity and reducing acne.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in aloe vera neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and lead to clogged pores, helping to keep pores clean and open.
Soothing and calming: Aloe vera's soothing and calming properties help reduce irritation and discomfort, allowing the skin to relax and open up, making it easier for pores to function properly.
remedy_constituents: Furocoumarins: These compounds help to open pores by increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation in the skin, allowing for a deeper cleansing and detoxification.

Alkaloids (specifically, aloin and aloe-emodin): These compounds act as a natural vasodilator, helping to relax the skin's blood vessels and increase blood flow, which in turn helps to open up pores and promote detoxification.

Phenolic acids (specifically, aloin and aloe-emodin): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the removal of toxins and impurities from the skin, allowing for a deeper cleansing and opening of pores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are used due to their high concentration of aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to open pores and improve skin clarity.
Rhyzomes: Aloe vera rhyzomes are used due to their rich content of anthraquinones, which aid in detoxifying and opening up the skin pores.
Stems: Aloe vera stems are used due to their high levels of vitamins and minerals, which help to nourish and open up the skin pores for improved skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe vera leaves with thick gel inside and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and then dry them in a warm place for 7 to 10 days.
Combine the dried aloe vera pieces with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part aloe to 3 parts vodka.
Store the mixture in a glass jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the herbal tincture to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree tinctures helps with open pores because it possesses natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to shrink the size of pores, giving the skin a smoother appearance. The tannins present in tea tree oil also have an astringent effect on the skin, helping to tighten and constrict pores, reducing their appearance of being clogged or enlarged. Additionally, the antifungal and antibacterial properties of tea tree oil help to prevent bacterial growth and inflammation, which can contribute to open pores.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil, a key ingredient in many herbal tea tree tinctures, has been shown to combat bacteria that can clog pores and cause acne, allowing pores to remain open and clear.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea tree tinctures often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and eucalyptus, which help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, keeping pores open and reducing the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads.

Astringent properties: Tannins found in herbal tea tree tinctures can help shrink the size of pores, making them appear smaller and more refined, while also tightening the skin to prevent excess oil and impurities from entering the pore.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps to open pores by increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation, which allows pores to breathe more efficiently.
Linalool: Works as a natural vasodilator, helping to widen blood vessels and increase blood flow to the skin's surface, thus opening pores.
Limonene: Exhibits a mild astringent effect, helping to balance the skin's pH and reducing oiliness, which can help to unclog pores and make them appear more open.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which helps to open pores and promote skin clarity.
Barks: The barks of the tree are known to contain bioactive compounds that aid in skin detoxification and pore opening.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain antimicrobial properties, which help to clean and open pores, reducing the appearance of acne and blackheads.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at the peak of potency and store them in a cool dry place immediately.
Chop the fresh leaves into small pieces using a sharp blade to release the active ingredients quickly.
Mix 100 grams of the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking every day.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle to create the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with open pores because its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties stimulate blood flow to the skin, allowing for a deeper cleansing of the pores. The natural oils in rosemary also help to regulate sebum production, reducing clogged pores and preventing future blockages. Additionally, rosemary's antibacterial properties combat acne-causing bacteria, further refining the appearance of open pores. As a result, the skin appears smoother, more radiant, and better able to breathe, giving it a healthier overall complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help protect the skin from free radical damage, which can cause pores to shrink and become clogged.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, reduce inflammation in the skin, which can cause pores to appear larger and more prominent.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Rosemary's vasoconstrictive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the skin, which can help open up pores and improve their appearance.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Helps to open pores by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, which in turn helps to clear pores of impurities and reduce sebum production.
Camphor: Opens pores by creating a cooling effect, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote the removal of dead skin cells, leading to clearer pores.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Assists in opening pores by stimulating circulation and promoting lymphatic drainage, which helps to remove toxins and excess oils from the skin, thereby reducing pore size.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils help to stimulate blood flow and open pores.
Stems: They contain compounds that aid in improving circulation and detoxification.
Flowers: Their essential oils help to relax muscles, reduce stress, and promote sweating, which can open pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves on a dry sunny morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area for tincture.
Combine 1 cup of chopped rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with open pores because it contains flavonoids, which have been shown to strengthen skin elasticity and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants in pot marigold also help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, allowing for a more even and balanced complexion. As a result, the pores appear smaller and less noticeable, giving the skin a smoother and more radiant appearance. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to reduce acne-causing bacteria, further improving the overall health and clarity of the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with clogged pores, allowing for a clearer and more even-toned complexion.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold tincture work to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the skin and lead to clogged pores, promoting healthy and radiant skin.

Astringent and tightening properties: Pot marigold's astringent and tightening properties help to constrict and shrink pores, giving the appearance of smaller, more refined pores and reducing the likelihood of blackheads and whiteheads.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help open pores by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the skin, allowing pores to clear and breathe more effectively.

Terpenoids (specifically, triterpenoids): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pore congestion and promote the removal of dead skin cells, allowing pores to open up.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote collagen production, allowing pores to appear smaller and more even.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis for making tinctures to open pores due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis are used in tinctures to help open pores due to their ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis are also used in tinctures to aid in opening pores, as they contain compounds that have a cooling effect and can help reduce heat and inflammation in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part of dried calendula flowers and leaves with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep the mixture for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium tinctures helps with open pores because it is a natural toner that balances the skin's pH levels. The tannins present in geranium essential oil help to tighten and shrink the pores, reducing their appearance on the skin surface. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of geranium oil work to purify the skin, eliminating dirt and impurities that can clog pores and lead to their opening up. This results in a smoother, more refined complexion with smaller-looking pores.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Geranium tincture has astringent properties that help to tighten and shrink pores, reducing their appearance and helping to minimize acne and blackheads.

Antiseptic and antibacterial properties: Geranium's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin, which can clog pores and cause acne, while also preventing infection and inflammation.

Tonic and balancing properties: Geranium tincture has a tonic effect on the skin, helping to balance the body's natural pH levels and regulating the production of sebum, which can help to unclog pores and reduce oiliness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to open pores by inducing vasodilation, which is the widening of blood vessels and capillaries to increase blood flow to the skin.
Geraniol: A terpene that helps to improve skin microcirculation and reduce inflammation, allowing pores to open and promoting a smoother skin texture.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce pore size and improve skin elasticity, allowing pores to open and appear more refined.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part to make tinctures for open pores due to their high concentration of essential oils that help to purify and balance the skin.
Stems: The stems of Pelargonium graveolens contain a rich amount of essential oils that help to reduce the appearance of pores and balance the skin's natural oils.
Buds: The buds of the plant are rich in essential oils that help to tone and purify the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and promoting a healthy complexion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and flowers from pelargonium graveolens plants in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Weigh 250 grams of the fresh plant material and add it to a clean glass jar.
Combine 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka with the plant material in the glass jar and seal it tightly.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and antiseptic compounds, which help to soothe and purify the skin, reducing oil production and opening pores.
Buds: Contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and bacteria that can clog pores, promoting healthy skin and open pores.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers weighing about 250 grams to ensure potency.
Combine the dried flowers with 75 percent vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:3 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar to remove solids.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used to make tinctures because they contain thymol, a compound with astringent and antiseptic properties that help to reduce oiliness and tighten pores.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife to ensure maximum surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of 25% glycerin and 75% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for open pores because they contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing.

Stems: The stems of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for open pores because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote collagen production.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day in june and july.

Use a 1:2 ratio of achillea millefolium to 95% ethanol and soak the mixture for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth and discard the solids after 24 hours of gentle squeezing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with open pores because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The apigenin and bisabolol present in chamomile have been shown to reduce inflammation, redness, and irritation that can cause pores to appear larger. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects help to calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pores by smoothing out their texture and making them less visible. remedy_parts: Flowers: These are the most commonly used part of Matricaria chamomilla to make tinctures for open pores, as they contain high levels of chamazulene and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when the buds are still tightly closed.
Combine the harvested flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean dark glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark location for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it every 2 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion root and leaves contain compounds like flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids that stimulate urine production, helping to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body, which can clog pores and make them appear smaller.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion tinctures, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to pore congestion, making it more difficult for pores to "breathe" and function properly.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale, such as quercetin, may help open pores by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation, allowing for a deeper cleansing of the skin.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid in Taraxacum officinale may aid in opening pores by reducing sebum production and killing bacteria that can clog pores, promoting a clearer complexion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200 fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the fall or spring when the plant is at its peak potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion and extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 80% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight and place in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the tincture from the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with open pores because it contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms the skin. When applied topically or ingested as a supplement, slippery elm helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin, allowing pores to shrink and tighten. Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tannins and flavonoids present in slippery elm have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with clogged pores, allowing them to open up and function properly.

Demulcent properties: Slippery elm's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the skin that locks in moisture and soothes dryness, helping to balance the skin's natural pH and open up pores.

remedy_recipe: Harvest ulmus rubra bark and flowers from a trusted source for quality and freshness.
Cleanse and dry the harvested ulmus rubra plant material in a warm oven or by air drying.
Combine 10 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark and 5 grams of dried ulmus rubra flowers with 250 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily during the first two weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids after allowing it to sit for 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with open pores because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with clogged pores, allowing for a more even skin tone.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have been shown to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the skin, helping to unclog pores and promote healthy skin cell turnover.
Liquiritin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria that can cause acne and other skin issues, allowing pores to remain clear and open.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to create a calming and soothing tincture that helps to reduce inflammation and open up congested pores.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly with cold running water.
Slice the cleaned roots into thin pieces and soak them in a mixture of 70% ethanol and distilled water at a ratio of 1:5 for 7 days.
Steep the root pieces in the ethanol-water mixture for an additional 7 days, allowing the liquid to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the tincture to mature in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it topically as a pore-opening treatment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis cuttings weighing approximately 100g.
Rinse the harvested root material thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt debris.
Chop the cleaned root into small pieces and combine with 700ml of high-proof vodka or rum spirits.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks allowing the herbs to infuse slowly.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich compounds in ginger tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, leading to clogged pores and other skincare issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots measuring approximately 10-12 inches long.
Clean the roots thoroughly by gently scrubbing them under running cool water.
Chop the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with open pores because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric root rhizome curcuma longa, washing thoroughly to remove dirt debris.
Dry the turmeric root in a low-temperature oven or dehydrator for 24 hours at 150°f.
Grind the dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 part ground turmeric with 2 parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a glass jar, sealing tightly.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard for 14 days, shaking daily to infuse the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with open pores because of its ability to stimulate blood flow and dilate the capillaries, allowing for a deeper cleanse and detoxification of the skin. Combine 250 grams of chopped fresh peppermint leaves with 500 milliliters of 95% grain alcohol.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days under shade and gentle stirring twice daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for a minimum of 30 days before using.

Combine the herb with 40% vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for open pores are concentrated plant extracts that help to reduce the appearance of enlarged pores by toning and tightening the skin. They work by balancing the skin's pH, reducing inflammation, and promoting collagen production. The best herbal tinctures for open pores include Hamamelis virginiana, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Silybum marianum, Melaleuca alternifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Achillea millefolium, Thymus vulgaris, Geranium maculatum, and Saponaria officinalis. If left untreated, open pores can lead to premature aging, acne, and low self-esteem, affecting a person's confidence and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to open pores that are treated with herbal tinctures are acne, eczema, rosacea, and minor skin irritations. Herbal tinctures such as tea tree oil, lavender, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the skin. These natural remedies can reduce redness, inflammation, and itching associated with these conditions, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with open pores is a blend of Calendula, Witch Hazel, and Green Tea. Calendula promotes skin healing, reduces inflammation, and closes pores. Witch Hazel helps to tighten and shrink pores, while also reducing redness and inflammation. Green Tea, rich in antioxidants, soothes and calms the skin, reducing the appearance of pores. When combined, these tinctures create a potent blend that not only heals open pores but also promotes overall skin health and balance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana tinctures helps with open pores because of its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the plant help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, which can cause pores to appear smaller. The tincture also has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with open pores. By balancing the skin's pH and reducing inflammation, Hamamelis virginiana tinctures can help to create a healthier and more even-toned complexion. It can also aid in reducing the appearance of acne and other skin issues.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help to open pores by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation, which allows for better sweat and toxin release.
Phenolic acids (specifically, ellagic acid): They help to open pores by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that cause skin to become thickened and clogged, promoting a clearer complexion.
Gallic acid: It helps to open pores by acting as an astringent, reducing sebum production and inflammation, and allowing for better skin detoxification.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used because they contain a high concentration of hamamelitannin, a key compound responsible for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a high amount of flavonoids, including hamamelitannin, which helps to reduce pore size and improve skin tone.
Stems: The stems are used because they also contain hamamelitannin and other flavonoids, making them a valuable resource for tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs from a trusted source in the morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer on paper bags at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka at a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the plant and vodka mixture in a glass jar with a lid in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then store in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with open pores because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work together to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation. As a result, the pores appear smaller and more refined, giving the skin a smoother and more even texture. Additionally, Calendula's antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial growth, keeping the pores clear and preventing clogged pores. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help to open pores by reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of dead skin cells.
Flavonoids: They assist in opening pores by improving blood circulation and reducing oxidative stress.
Phenolic acids: They help to open pores by exerting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, allowing for a clearer complexion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are used to make tinctures due to their high content of triterpene saponins, which help to break down sebum and open pores.

Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis are used to make tinctures as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to tighten pores and reduce inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce pore size.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny morning just after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with open pores because of its potent aloe vera extract, rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe barbadensis leaves when mature and firm, typically after 3 to 6 months of growth.

Chop the aloe leaves into small pieces to release the active ingredients and facilitate tincture extraction.
Combine the chopped aloe pieces with a 1:2 ratio of aloe to 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the container occasionally to enhance extraction.

Combine 1 part silybum marianum powder with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.

Clean and dry the harvested roots to prevent contamination and spoilage.
